title: Luxuriant Juicy Korean Custard Tang
description: A pungent and juicy Korean dish featuring custard, toast, and anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients, traditionally served in the Gyeongsang region as a hangover cure.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine eggs, milk, gochujang, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, and turmeric, and blend until smooth.
Pour the mixture into 4 ramekins or small cups.
Place the ramekins in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
While the custard is baking, toast the bread until lightly browned.
Remove the custard from the water bath and let cool to room temperature.
To serve, place a slice of toast in the bottom of a bowl, top with a ramekin of custard, and garnish with green onions and a drizzle of sesame oil.
